title: Values should not be uselessly incremented
description: Values should not be uselessly incrementedAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugunusedWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoWhen using thepostfix incrementoperator, it is important to know that the result of the expressionx++is the valuebeforethe operationx.This means that in some cases, the result might not be what you expect:When assigningx++tox, it’s the same as assigningxto itself, since the value is assigned before the increment takes placeWhen returningx++, the returning value isx, notx+1The same applies to the postfix and prefixdecrementoperators.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since9.1Analyze code in youron-premi
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects postfix increment/decrement in return statements or self-assignments like 'x = x++'

checks.message: Useless postfix increment in return statement - returns value before increment
Useless postfix decrement in return statement - returns value before decrement
Useless self-assignment with postfix increment - variable value unchanged
Useless self-assignment with postfix decrement - variable value unchanged

logic.triggering_conditions: return x++; OR x = x++; OR return x--; OR x = x--;
logic.reasoning: Postfix increment/decrement returns the value BEFORE the operation. When used in 'return x++;' the original value is returned and increment is lost. When used in 'x = x++;' the assignment overwrites the increment. These are almost always bugs.
